name: FAB
summary: A persuasion framework that links Features to Advantages and then to the buyer-facing Benefits that actually matter in the deal.
whenToUseIt: Use FAB in demos, proposals, and stakeholder conversations when you need to translate product capability into practical buyer value without sounding overly technical.
steps: Name the feature or capability clearly.
Explain the practical advantage it creates in workflow, speed, control, or reliability.
Translate that advantage into the business benefit the buyer actually cares about.
example: A manager dashboard is the feature. The advantage is real-time visibility into rep activity and pipeline movement. The benefit is faster coaching and more accurate weekly forecasts.
